_README: Reviewed allowlist of PROVEN, already-applied duplicate migration-version collisions. A file appears here ONLY after: (1) full SHA-256 pin, (2) an independent durable apply receipt (risk_alerts alert_type='migration_apply'), (3) a distinct production schema_migrations version (apply-timestamp assigned by mcp apply_migration), (4) an object-parity note confirming disjoint DDL from its siblings, and (5) never_reapply=true. The linter (scripts/migrations/migration_version_audit.py, enforced by packages/quantum/tests/test_migration_version_collision.py) PASSES a duplicate version only when every on-disk file in the group matches an entry here by exact hash. NEVER rename, reapply, or squash an applied file. To ADD a legacy collision, verify each fact against production and record the receipt id here; to PREVENT a new collision, rename the not-yet-applied file to a unique 14-digit prefix BEFORE applying it.
_doctrine_refs: docs/migration_procedure.md (apply by exact NAME via mcp apply_migration; DO NOT use supabase db push)
CLAUDE.md section 8: match files to tracking by NAME not version prefix
CLAUDE.md section 10: model identity and deploy identity are different provenance axes

collisions.reason: Three parallel research / shadow-fleet lanes each authored a migration with the same 14-digit prefix on 2026-07-23 (PRs #1364 / #1365 / #1366, merged as commits 55be29b3 / 6883a422 / b96107a3). The files create pairwise-DISJOINT object sets and were each applied to production by exact NAME via mcp apply_migration, receiving three distinct MCP-assigned schema_migrations versions and three independent apply receipts. Production history therefore has NO version collision; the collision is strictly repo-side (the Supabase CLI keys local migration identity on the 14-digit filename prefix, so `supabase migration list --local` shows this version three times, and the local schema_migrations PRIMARY KEY is `version` alone). VERIFIED via SELECT against production schema_migrations + risk_alerts and `supabase migration list --local` (CLI 2.72.7) on 2026-07-23/24.
collisions.cli_collision: BLOCKED_TOOLING_COLLISION: a fresh `supabase db push` / `db reset` cannot record all three (version-PK conflict). The repo's supported bootstrap is name-based per-file apply, under which each file lands exactly once (production proof below).
collisions.object_parity: Pairwise-disjoint. td_scan_observe_tables -> {td_scan_envelopes, td_scan_scores}; regime_v4_comparisons -> {regime_v4_comparisons}; fleet_policy_decision_foundation -> {fleet_policy_decision_runs, fleet_policy_decisions}. No shared table / index / function / trigger / policy name across the three. All five tables confirmed present in production.
